rag_rules: AGREE assumptions must be placed in component type scopes when referring to component ports
AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
generic_rules: Component type annexes are the standard location for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
Use valid AADL model structure as the authoritative source for component names, ports, and data types
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be syntactically valid within annex blocks
Helper declarations must align types with actual expressions (e.g., bool for comparisons)
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicit typed ports or constants are available
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names as AGREE variables
Boolean data range comparisons with opaque types
Model fusion and owner/scope preservation rules
